claim_chain: We used TurboID to uncover the electrical synapse proteome in retinal neurons
Our screen identified a plethora of molecules associated with neuronal connexins
We identified Sipa1l3 as a novel scaffold protein for electrical synapses
The presence of ZO proteins and endocytosis components suggests conservation in the proteome
Cell type-specific differences may exist in the Cx36 interactome
Our study provides the first detailed view of the electrical synapse proteome in retinal neurons

typical_pattern: Opening statement of what was done or found
Elaboration of findings with specific examples
Forward-looking statement about future work needed

likely_priorities: Validation of novel interactors through independent methods
Mechanistic understanding of protein function at electrical synapses
likely_attacks: Lack of functional validation for Sipa1l3
Uncertainty about exact gap junction channel configuration in zebrafish photoreceptors
Unknown mechanism of how Sipa1l3 influences electrical synapse formation
Absence of Mupp1 in AII amacrine cell interactome requires explanation
Need for knockout studies to determine exact protein functions
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states multiple times that further studies are needed: 'To determine the exact function of Sipa1l3, further studies on KO mice will be required'; 'How exactly these gap junction channels in zebrafish photoreceptors are configured and which exact cell types they connect remains to be determined'; 'How and when it is determined where electrical synapses are formed is currently unknown'. The paper also raises questions about mechanisms: 'This raises the question of what potential function Sipa1l3 might serve' and 'One way in which Sipa1l3 might influence the formation of electrical synapses might be as a regulator'. The discussion of Mupp1 absence and cell-type differences also suggests reviewers would question the completeness and generalizability of findings.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. The profile accurately reflects the paper's methodological focus, moderate hedging, and balanced claim strength.
